data.title: Analysis: International oil prices remain high, and the potential blockade of the Strait of Hormuz remains a major concern.
data.description: International oil prices remained high, with Brent crude futures holding steady above $100 a barrel, despite US measures to temporarily allow countries to purchase Russian oil already at sea to increase global supply. Swissquote analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya stated, "These quick measures are unlikely to alleviate the pressure." The main concern in the oil market remains the potential blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. US Treasury Secretary Bessenter called for the formation of an international coalition to escort oil tankers through the strait "as soon as military conditions permit," despite Iran's new leader's vow to continue the blockade of the waterway. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysts: Growing market concerns about the Fed's interest rate cut path will likely lead to a weekly decline in gold prices.
data.description: Gold futures in New York fell below $5,100 per ounce on Friday, setting the stage for a weekly decline. A stronger dollar, rising oil prices, and uncertainty surrounding Federal Reserve policy weighed on gold prices. ANZ analysts stated, "The dollar strengthened due to its safe-haven status, particularly as rising oil prices benefited the US, a net energy exporter. Growing concerns about the Fed's interest rate path and rising oil prices have increased the likelihood of renewed inflationary pressures." (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum Block Builder Extracts $34 Million from DeFi Swap Mishap
data.description: Ethereum block builder Titan Builder extracted $34 million from a failed DeFi swap, briefly becoming the top protocol by daily revenue. According to NS3.AI, the trader attempted to swap aEthUSDT into aEthAAVE via CoW Protocol but ended up with only about $35,000 due to extreme slippage and a sandwich attack. This incident has reignited discussions on whether DeFi front ends adequately protect users from significant execution failures.

data.title: Dollar Index Surges Amid Rising Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
The dollar index has surpassed the 100 mark for the first time since November last year, climbing over 0.3% and extending its gains for the fourth consecutive day. According to RTHK, analysts predict that the dollar index may continue its upward trend for a second consecutive week. The yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury note recently rose to nearly 4.28%.

Additionally, the dollar strengthened against the yen, approaching the 160 yen level, with a recent report showing it at 159.38 yen. The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has prompted a shift of funds from risk assets like stocks into safe-haven assets such as the dollar and U.S. Treasuries.

Market concerns are growing over the potential for a sharp rise in energy prices to significantly increase inflation, which could lead to major central banks raising interest rates within the year.

data.title: Southeast Asia's Scam Centers Exploiting Workers Revealed
data.description: In Southeast Asia, tens of thousands of workers have been trafficked into scam centers resembling prisons, where they are coerced into convincing people globally to invest in fraudulent schemes or send money to fictitious romantic partners. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing that until recently, the operations within these compounds were largely unknown. However, late last year, the Thai military intervened and took control of a significant scam complex in Cambodia during military clashes along the shared border between the two countries. This development has shed light on the inner workings of these exploitative centers.

data.title: Star Capital Launches Share Offering for Vincorion Valued at €850 Million
data.description: Star Capital is set to offer 17.65 million shares at a price of €17 each, valuing Vincorion at a market capitalization of €850 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial move by the investment firm. This offering marks a significant step for Vincorion, positioning it for potential growth and expansion in the market. The valuation reflects investor confidence in the company's future prospects.

data.title: Euro Hits Seven-Month Low Against Dollar
data.description: The euro fell by 0.4% to $1.1467, marking its lowest level in over seven months. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the currency's decline amid ongoing economic uncertainties. The drop comes as investors react to various factors influencing the global financial markets, including shifts in monetary policy and geopolitical tensions. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation to assess potential impacts on international trade and investment strategies.

data.title: China's Central Bank Strengthens Yuan Fixing Amid Economic Developments
data.description: The People's Bank of China has adjusted the yuan fixing, strengthening it by 0.03% this week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this move as part of ongoing economic strategies. The adjustment reflects China's efforts to manage its currency amid global financial fluctuations. This decision comes as the country navigates various economic challenges and opportunities, aiming to stabilize its financial markets and maintain economic growth. The yuan's fixing is a critical tool used by the central bank to influence the currency's value and ensure stability in the foreign exchange market.

data.title: The sixth round of China-US trade consultations will be held.
data.description: Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun hosted a regular press conference. A new round of China-US trade consultations will be held in Paris this weekend. Spokesperson, what topics will be discussed? What are China's expectations for these consultations? In response, Guo Jiakun stated, "China has already released information about the sixth round of China-US trade consultations; you can refer to it. For specific questions, I suggest contacting the relevant Chinese authorities." (CCTV News)

data.title: Three newly created wallets withdrew 2.54 million Trump tokens from Binance in the past 12 hours, worth $8.8 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, three newly created wallets withdrew 2.54 million TRUMP from Binance in the past 12 hours, worth $8.8 million.

data.title: Ed Yardeni Warns of Potential Stock Market Sell-Off Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Ed Yardeni has expressed concerns about a potential sharp sell-off in the stock market, estimating a 35% likelihood as tensions in the Middle East persist. According to NS3.AI, Yardeni highlighted that historically, oil shocks have often preceded recessions and bear markets. He also noted that while a 10% to 15% market correction is possible, a bear market would entail a decline of 20% or more.

data.title: Significant Bitcoin Transfer Observed Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A substantial Bitcoin transaction was recorded involving anonymous addresses. According to ChainCatcher, at 15:08 UTC+8, 594.13 BTC were transferred from one anonymous address (starting with bc1qkpas) to another (starting with bc1qf7tz). Following this, a portion of the Bitcoin, specifically 3 BTC, was moved to Nobitex.ir.

data.title: Romania's Inflation Eases but Energy Prices Pose Challenges to Monetary Policy
data.description:
Romania's inflation rate has eased in line with expectations, but the surge in global energy prices threatens this trend and may hinder the central bank's ability to ease monetary policy in the short term. According to Jin10, the National Institute of Statistics reported on Friday that the consumer price index rose by 9.3% year-on-year in February, down from 9.6% in the previous month. This figure aligns with the median forecast from surveys. On a month-to-month basis, prices increased by 0.6%.

Due to financial market volatility and soaring inflation, the Romanian central bank has maintained its benchmark interest rate at 6.5%—the highest within the European Union—for over a year and a half as a pillar of economic stability. With the country's economy now in recession and oil prices indicating further increases in fuel costs, the prospect of a potential rate cut has become increasingly uncertain.

data.title: Thailand's Economic Growth Faces Challenges Amid Global Tensions
data.description:
Thailand's economy is expected to face further slowdown this year due to the combined impact of a weakened tourism sector, declining exports, and rising energy prices. According to Jin10, the University of the Thai Chamber of Commerce, a leading private forecasting institution, estimates that if tensions in the Middle East persist for three months, Thailand's GDP growth rate could be halved from the previously predicted 2%. Last year, the country's economic growth rate was 2.4%. In a more severe scenario, if the conflict extends to six months, the growth rate could decrease by as much as 2.3 percentage points, potentially leading to an economic contraction. This would mark the first annual decline since the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020.

Tourism is seen as the most direct channel of impact. Thailand's target of attracting 36.7 million foreign tourists this year is increasingly at risk, with visitors from Europe and the Middle East accounting for about a quarter of the total. These tourists are likely to reduce their travel plans. The effects may extend beyond tourism. As shipping disruptions affect routes to Europe, Thai exporters are also facing ripple effects. Industries such as automotive and machinery, which rely heavily on European and Middle Eastern markets, are particularly vulnerable to these shocks.

data.title: UK Economy Stagnates in January Amid Global Uncertainties
data.description: The UK economy showed no growth in January, indicating a stall in activity prior to potential impacts from the Middle East conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the economic stagnation could be exacerbated by geopolitical tensions, affecting both consumers and businesses. The lack of growth raises questions about the resilience of the UK economy in the face of global uncertainties.

data.title: Japan's Defense Minister Considers Escorting Japanese Vessels
data.description: Japan's Defense Minister has indicated that providing escort services for Japanese ships is a possibility. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid growing regional tensions and security concerns. The minister emphasized the importance of safeguarding maritime interests and ensuring the safety of Japanese vessels in international waters. This development reflects Japan's proactive stance in addressing potential threats and maintaining secure navigation routes for its maritime activities.

data.title: GBP/USD Declines Following UK GDP Data Release
data.description: The British pound fell against the U.S. dollar, reaching an intraday low of 1.3305 after the release of the UK's GDP data. According to Jin10, the currency's movement reflects market reactions to the economic indicators. The GDP figures have raised concerns among investors, influencing the pound's performance in the forex market. Traders are closely monitoring the situation as they assess the potential impact on future monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Kia Recalls 14,870 Vehicles in the U.S. Market
data.description: Kia America has announced a recall of 14,870 vehicles in the United States, according to Jin10. The recall is being conducted under the supervision of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). The specific models and reasons for the recall have not been disclosed. Vehicle owners are advised to contact Kia for further information and instructions on how to proceed. This recall is part of Kia's ongoing commitment to ensuring the safety and reliability of its vehicles in the market.

data.title: The US dollar index (DXY) has returned to the 100 mark for the first time since November of last year.
data.description: The US Dollar Index (DXY) has returned to the 100 mark for the first time since November of last year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Oil Price Surge Offers Algeria Economic Relief Amid Global Tensions
data.description:
Algeria is experiencing economic relief following the surge in energy prices triggered by the Russia-Ukraine conflict. According to Jin10, the North African nation, with a population of 47 million, has long relied on its substantial oil and gas reserves to fund various subsidies. Since the oil price crash in 2014, Algeria has struggled to address budget deficits. On March 9, oil prices exceeded $100 per barrel, and despite subsequent fluctuations, prices have risen over 50% due to concerns that the conflict may disrupt Middle Eastern supply.

Independent economic and financial analyst Mahfoud Kaoubi noted that the price increase is beneficial for Algeria's authorities. If oil prices further rise to the $120-$125 range, the OPEC member could achieve budget balance. Currently, Algeria's daily oil production stands at approximately 977,000 barrels.
